description: Improve opentabs doctor diagnostics (npm plugin checks, MCP client check, fix false alarm) and opentabs audit (ANSI alignment, --tool filter, --since filter).

userStories.title: Fix doctor false alarm for npm-only users
userStories.description: As a user with only npm-installed plugins, I don't want to see a warning about having no local plugins configured.
acceptanceCriteria: When localPlugins is empty, doctor shows an info-level message (dim, not yellow warning) saying 'No local plugins configured (npm plugins are auto-discovered at server startup)'
The message does not use the yellow '!' icon — it uses a dim '-' or is omitted entirely
When localPlugins has entries, the per-plugin checks remain unchanged
userStories.notes: Edit platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts checkPlugins function (lines 153-211). Currently lines 161-169 return a warn() when pluginPaths.length === 0. Change this to return a pass() or an info-level result. Since CheckResult only has ok/fatal states, returning pass('Local plugins', 'none configured (npm plugins are auto-discovered at startup)') would be the simplest fix — it shows a green check instead of a yellow warning.

userStories.title: Add npm plugin health check to doctor
userStories.description: As a user, I want doctor to verify that my npm-installed plugins are healthy when the server is running.
acceptanceCriteria: When the MCP server is reachable, doctor fetches /health and checks pluginDetails for npm-sourced plugins
Each npm plugin is listed with its name, version, and status (ready/unavailable/closed)
Failed plugins from /health are shown as warnings with their error message
When the server is not running, npm plugin checks are skipped with a dim note: 'npm plugin health requires a running server'
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts. After checkPlugins (local plugin checks), add a new check function that uses the health data already fetched in checkServerHealth (the healthData variable on line 225).

Add a new function checkNpmPlugins(data: Record<string, unknown> | null): CheckResult[]. If data is null (server not reachable), return [info('npm plugins', 'requires running server to check')]. If data has pluginDetails, filter for source === 'npm' and create pass/warn results based on tabState. Also check failedPlugins array for any failures.

Insert the call after line 240 (after local plugin checks):
```
results.push(...checkNpmPlugins(healthData));
```

userStories.title: Add MCP client configuration check to doctor
userStories.description: As a user, I want doctor to check if my MCP client is configured to connect to OpenTabs.
acceptanceCriteria: Doctor checks for Claude Code config at ~/.claude/settings/mcp.json and reports if an 'opentabs' entry exists
Doctor checks for Cursor config at .cursor/mcp.json in the current directory
If at least one MCP client config is found with an opentabs entry, shows a green check
If no MCP client configs are found, shows a yellow warning with setup instructions
The check does not fail fatally (just warns) since the user might use an unsupported client
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts. Add a new checkMcpClientConfig function. Check these paths:
1. ~/.claude/settings/mcp.json (Claude Code) — use join(homedir(), '.claude', 'settings', 'mcp.json')
2. .cursor/mcp.json (Cursor) — use join(process.cwd(), '.cursor', 'mcp.json')

For each file that exists, try to parse JSON and check if it has mcpServers.opentabs. Return pass if found, warn if not found in any location.

Insert the check after the extension checks and before plugin checks. The label should be 'MCP client config'.

userStories.title: Fix audit table ANSI column alignment
userStories.description: As a user, I want the audit table columns to be properly aligned regardless of color codes.
acceptanceCriteria: The status column (checkmark/cross) has consistent visual alignment across all rows
The padEnd operation accounts for ANSI escape code length so visual width is correct
Rows with green checkmarks and red crosses align to the same column position

userStories.title: Add --tool filter to opentabs audit
userStories.description: As a user, I want to filter audit entries by tool name to debug specific tool invocations.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'opentabs audit --tool slack_send_message' filters to only entries for that tool
The --tool parameter is passed as a query parameter to the /audit endpoint
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/cli/src/commands/audit.ts.
1. Add to AuditOptions interface (line 11): tool?: string
2. In handleAudit (line 75): if (options.tool) url.searchParams.set('tool', options.tool)
3. In registerAuditCommand: add .option('--tool <name>', 'Filter by tool name')
4. Add to help text examples: '  $ opentabs audit --tool slack_send_message'

The server already supports the 'tool' query parameter (platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts line 308).

userStories.title: Add --since and --before time filters to opentabs audit
userStories.description: As a user, I want to filter audit entries by time range to see what happened during a specific period.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'opentabs audit --since 1h' shows entries from the last hour
Running 'opentabs audit --since 30m' shows entries from the last 30 minutes
Running 'opentabs audit --since 2d' shows entries from the last 2 days
Supported suffixes: s (seconds), m (minutes), h (hours), d (days)
Client-side filtering of entries based on their timestamp field
Invalid duration format produces a clear error message
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/cli/src/commands/audit.ts. Since the server /audit endpoint doesn't support time filtering, do it client-side after receiving entries.

1. Add to AuditOptions: since?: string
2. Add option: .option('--since <duration>', 'Show entries from the last duration (e.g., 1h, 30m, 2d)')
3. Add a parseDuration function that parses '30m' -> 30*60*1000, '1h' -> 3600000, '2d' -> 172800000, etc.
4. After fetching entries, filter: entries = entries.filter(e => new Date(e.timestamp).getTime() >= Date.now() - durationMs)
5. When --since is used, request a higher limit from the server (e.g., 500, the max) to ensure we have enough entries to filter from.

Add examples to help text.
